purpose.summary: This COBOL program contains routines for inserting child segments into a GSAM database, closing files, and handling abends. It uses IMS calls via CBLTDLI to interact with the database.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
inputs.description: Data to be inserted into the GSAM database as a child segment.

called_programs.purpose: To perform IMS database operations, specifically inserting a child segment.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph inserts a child segment into a GSAM database. It calls the CBLTDLI routine with the ISRT function to insert the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment using the PADFLPCB. After the call, it checks the PADFL-PCB-STATUS. If the status is not spaces, it displays error messages including the PCB status and the key feedback area, and then performs 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. The paragraph consumes the PADFLPCB and PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS as input. It produces no direct output other than potentially triggering an abend. It calls CBLTDLI to insert the segment and 9999-ABEND in case of an error.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 3200-INSERT-CHILD-SEG-GSAM paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement, providing a common point to return from the paragraph. It consumes no input and produces no output. Its primary purpose is to provide a structured exit from the 3200-INSERT-CHILD-SEG-GSAM paragraph, ensuring proper control flow. It does not perform any business logic or error handling. It does not call any other paragraphs or programs.

This paragraph is responsible for closing files. Currently, it only displays a message 'CLOSING THE FILE'. It does not take any input or produce any output other than the display message. The paragraph's purpose is to provide a centralized location for file closing operations, although the current implementation lacks the actual file closing logic. It does not implement any business logic or error handling beyond the display message. It does not call any other paragraphs or programs.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 4000-FILE-CLOSE paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement, providing a common point to return from the paragraph. It consumes no input and produces no output. Its primary purpose is to provide a structured exit from the 4000-FILE-CLOSE paragraph, ensuring proper control flow. It does not perform any business logic or error handling. It does not call any other paragraphs or programs.

This paragraph handles program termination (abend) scenarios. It displays a message 'DBUNLDGS ABENDING ...', sets the RETURN-CODE to 16, and then terminates the program using GOBACK. It takes no input other than the implicit program state. It produces no direct output other than the display message and the return code. The paragraph's purpose is to provide a centralized point for abnormal program termination, setting a specific return code for diagnostic purposes. It does not implement any specific business logic beyond setting the return code. It does not call any other paragraphs or programs.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 9999-ABEND paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement, providing a common point to return from the paragraph. It consumes no input and produces no output. Its primary purpose is to provide a structured exit, although in this case, it's unlikely to be reached since 9999-ABEND performs a GOBACK. It does not perform any business logic or error handling. It does not call any other paragraphs or programs.
